title: Read Replicas
description: Scale reads with replicas; handle replication lag and read-your-writes requirements.

cost.costNotes: Seeded via heuristics; validate against current pricing and expected workload (throughput, storage, retention, egress).

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: Read replicas require running additional database instances. Minimum assumes 1 primary + 1 read replica on small instances (e.g., AWS RDS db.t3.small ~$25/each = $50 total for dev/test). Maximum represents production scenarios with 1 primary + 3-5 read replicas on larger instances (e.g., db.r5.2xlarge ~$800-1000/each), plus cross-region data transfer costs ($0.02/GB), backup storage, and potential multi-AZ deployment. Geographic distribution and high-traffic applications can push costs higher. Does not include data storage costs which vary by volume.
provenance.adoptionCost: Medium-high complexity implementation. Requires: (1) Database replication setup and configuration (4-8 hours), (2) Application code changes to route reads to replicas while handling read-your-writes consistency (8-16 hours), (3) Implementing read/write splitting logic in connection pools or middleware (4-8 hours), (4) Handling replication lag scenarios and fallback mechanisms (8-12 hours), (5) Monitoring setup for replication lag and replica health (4-6 hours), (6) Load testing and optimization (8-12 hours), (7) Documentation and runbooks for operations (4-6 hours). Total ~40-68 hours at $50-75/hour blended rate. Requires understanding of database replication mechanics, eventual consistency patterns, and distributed systems concepts.
provenance.licenseCost: Read replica functionality is a standard feature in all major database platforms (PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, etc.) with no additional licensing fees. Cloud providers (AWS RDS, Azure Database, GCP Cloud SQL) include replication as a built-in feature - you only pay for the compute/storage of additional instances. Open-source databases support replication natively. No proprietary tools or commercial licenses required for basic implementation.

provides.reasoning: The primary purpose of read replicas is to scale read operations horizontally by distributing read traffic across multiple database copies.
Read replicas provide redundancy and fault tolerance for read operations, ensuring reads can continue even if one replica fails.
Read replicas inherently introduce replication lag between primary and replicas, resulting in eventual consistency for read operations across the system.
This pattern can implement read-your-writes logic by routing certain reads to the primary database or using session-based routing to ensure users see their own writes.
Read replicas ARE database replication — the pattern establishes and manages replication streams from primary to replica nodes.

requires.reasoning: Read replicas are typically implemented on top of a primary relational database like PostgreSQL or MySQL that supports replication.
The pattern fundamentally depends on the underlying database's ability to replicate data from primary to replica instances.
Requires a mechanism to distribute read queries across multiple replicas, either through application-level routing or database proxy load balancing.
Tracking replication lag is critical for this pattern's operational success to ensure data freshness meets application requirements and to detect replication issues.

supports_nfr.reason: Read replicas with automated failover can provide up to 99.99% availability for read traffic.
Relational database read operations through replicas have a minimum p95 latency of 5ms due to network and query execution overhead.
Replica routing and replication lag considerations result in a minimum p99 latency of 20ms for read operations.
Read traffic can failover to healthy replicas within 1-2 minutes using automated health checks and DNS updates.
Maximum throughput of 50M jobs/hour achievable with multi-region replica deployments and read distribution.
Read replicas are a scalability tool only beneficial when read QPS exceeds 500, justifying the operational complexity.
Read replicas work with any tenant isolation strategy as they faithfully replicate the primary database structure and data.
Read replicas are compatible with GDPR requirements when combined with appropriate encryption, access controls, and data residency configurations.
Read replicas support HIPAA-compliant deployments with proper encryption at rest and in transit, audit trails, and access controls.
Read replicas are compatible with CCPA compliance when combined with appropriate data governance and user data management capabilities.
Read replicas support SOX-compliant architectures with proper audit trails, transaction integrity, and change management controls.
Read replicas can support both eventual consistency and read-your-writes patterns through routing strategies like session affinity or sync reads from primary.
Read replicas support both strict and relaxed durability requirements depending on replication mode (synchronous vs asynchronous) configuration.

supports_constraints.reason: Read replicas are supported by all major cloud providers and on-premises database systems with native replication capabilities.
Read replicas are a database infrastructure pattern that works with any programming language using standard database drivers.
Read replicas are applicable to any platform that requires database read scalability and high availability.
Read replicas work with or without caching layers as they are complementary scalability strategies for read-heavy workloads.
Read replicas are compatible with both synchronous request-response and asynchronous messaging architectures.
Read replicas can serve AI inference workloads that require read-heavy database access for feature retrieval and model metadata.
Read replicas support both single-tenant and multi-tenant database architectures with any isolation strategy.
Read replicas are useful for offloading batch read operations and analytics queries from the primary database instance.
Read replicas can participate in distributed transaction architectures for read operations while writes remain on the primary.
Read replicas can serve real-time streaming applications with eventual consistency guarantees for read queries.
Read replicas can replicate vector search indexes for scaling vector similarity queries across multiple read instances.
Read replicas work with both relational and document-oriented databases that support database-level replication.
Read replicas can be implemented for key-value stores that support database-level replication capabilities.
Read replicas can scale read-heavy graph query workloads when the graph database supports replication.
Read replicas are effective for scaling time-series database read queries and analytical workloads.
Read replicas are primarily designed for scaling OLTP read-heavy workloads with transactional consistency requirements.
Read replicas can support OLAP workloads by offloading analytical queries from the primary transactional database.

replication_mode.description: Replication mode: asynchronous (fastest, eventual consistency), synchronous (slower, strong consistency), semi-synchronous (balance between performance and durability)

replica_count.description: Number of read replicas to provision. More replicas increase read capacity but add replication lag and cost

read_consistency.description: Read consistency level: eventual (read from any replica), session (read-your-writes within session), strong (read from primary for critical data)

routing_strategy.description: How to route read queries: manual (app controls), round-robin (distribute evenly), least-connections (load-based), geographic (nearest replica)

failover_behavior.description: Behavior when primary fails: read_only (replicas serve reads only), promote_replica (auto-promote to new primary), circuit_breaker (fail fast and retry)

requires_nfr.reason: Read replicas with automated failover can provide up to 99.99% availability for read traffic.
Relational database read operations through replicas have a minimum p95 latency of 5ms due to network and query execution overhead.
Replica routing and replication lag considerations result in a minimum p99 latency of 20ms for read operations.
Read traffic can failover to healthy replicas within 1-2 minutes using automated health checks and DNS updates.
Synchronous or near-synchronous replication to replicas provides near-zero RPO for committed transactions.
Maximum throughput of 50M jobs/hour achievable with multi-region replica deployments and read distribution.

warn_nfr.reason: Read replicas introduce replication lag (typically 10-1000ms). Supporting read-your-writes requires routing reads to primary or session affinity, reducing replica utilization and increasing complexity.
warn_nfr.message: db-read-replicas: spec requires needsReadYourWrites=true. Replication lag means reads must route to primary or use session affinity, reducing replica effectiveness.
